intro: Tracking KPIs shouldn't require a BI tool, a data team, or a week of setup. If your numbers live in a spreadsheet, you can build a KPI dashboard from CSV data in minutes. Distill is one of the fastest ways to do it: upload your file and get auto-generated charts, narratives, and a shareable dashboard instantly.

whatIs.heading: What Is a KPI Dashboard?
whatIs.body: A KPI dashboard is a single view that displays your most important performance metrics in one place. It typically combines charts, summary numbers, and trend lines so you can spot problems or progress at a glance. It works best with structured data that includes a time column, a metric name or category, and numeric values like revenue, conversion rate, or ticket count.

whenToUse.heading: When Should You Use a KPI Dashboard from CSV?
bullets: You export weekly or monthly metrics from tools like Stripe, HubSpot, or Google Analytics and need a quick visual summary for your team.
You are preparing a board update or stakeholder report and want clear charts instead of raw spreadsheet rows.
Your company doesn't have a dedicated BI platform yet, but leadership still needs to track performance against targets.

excelSteps.heading: How to Make a KPI Dashboard in Excel
steps: Open your CSV file in Excel. Make sure each column has a clear header like Date, Metric, and Value.
Create a summary table using formulas. Use SUMIFS or AVERAGEIFS to calculate each KPI's current value and compare it to a target or previous period.
Insert a chart for each KPI. Bar charts work well for comparisons, and line charts work well for trends over time. Select the relevant data range and choose Insert > Chart.
Add conditional formatting or icon sets to your summary table so readers can quickly see which KPIs are on track and which are not.
Arrange your charts and summary table onto a single worksheet. Resize and align them so everything fits on one screen without scrolling.
Save the workbook and share it. If you need to update it later, paste new CSV data into the source sheet and refresh your formulas and charts.

sheetsSteps.heading: How to Make a KPI Dashboard in Google Sheets
steps: Import your CSV by going to File > Import and selecting your file. Choose 'Replace current sheet' or 'Insert new sheet' depending on your preference.
Create a new sheet called Dashboard. Use QUERY or FILTER functions to pull summarized KPI values from your raw data sheet.
Add sparklines using the SPARKLINE function to show inline trend lines next to each KPI value.
Insert full charts by selecting a data range and clicking Insert > Chart. Pick chart types that match each metric: scorecards for single numbers, line charts for trends.
Use data validation and named ranges if you want dropdown filters, like selecting a specific month or department.
Share the Google Sheet with your team using the Share button. Set permissions to View Only if you want to prevent accidental edits to the dashboard.

distillSection.heading: A Faster Way: Skip the Manual Steps with Distill
distillSection.body: If building formulas and formatting charts feels like busywork, Distill removes all of it. Upload your CSV and Distill automatically detects your KPIs, generates relevant charts, writes plain-language narratives explaining the trends, and packages everything into a shareable dashboard. There is no pivot table to configure and no chart formatting to fiddle with. You get a finished KPI dashboard in seconds, not hours. It is especially useful when you need to turn a raw export into something presentable for a meeting or a report on short notice.

gifAlt: Screen recording showing a CSV file with monthly KPI data being uploaded to Distill, which then automatically generates a KPI dashboard with summary charts, trend lines, and written narratives.

faqs.q: Can I create a KPI dashboard without Excel?
faqs.a: Yes. Google Sheets is a solid free alternative that supports charts, sparklines, and shared editing. Distill is another option that skips the manual setup entirely. Just upload your CSV and get a finished dashboard with auto-generated visuals and narratives.

faqs.q: How should I structure my CSV for a KPI dashboard?
faqs.a: At a minimum, include a date or time period column, a column identifying the metric or KPI name, and a numeric value column. One row per metric per time period is the cleanest format. Avoid merged cells, blank rows, or inconsistent date formats, as these cause problems in every tool.
Yes. Distill's free tier supports CSV files up to 1,000 rows and includes all chart types. No account is required to try it.
